title: Orders — Iceberg table contract
description: Authoritative shape of the Iceberg table the learner must create. CI compares the schema and the partition spec against this contract exactly.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: Globally unique order id; primary key, never null.

columns.name: event_time
columns.description: Order timestamp (microsecond precision). The table is partitioned by year(event_time) — see partition_spec below.

columns.name: country
columns.description: ISO-2 country code of the buyer.

columns.name: unit_price_cents
columns.description: Unit price, in cents (avoids decimal-float surprises).

partition_spec.name: event_time_year
partition_spec.rationale: Hidden partitioning by year(event_time). One partition per year. Critical: this is NOT 'add an order_year column and partition by identity'. The transform lives in the partition spec, not in the schema.

notes: DO NOT add an `order_year` column to the schema. Iceberg's hidden partitioning derives the partition value from `event_time` at write time. That's the whole point of the project.
The table identifier in the REST catalog is `default.orders_v1`.
